items.title: Oil prices edge higher after Trump says Iran will pay for killing U.S. service members
items.description: The U.S. has bombed Iran for nine consecutive nights in retaliation for repeated attacks on oil tankers transiting the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Trump says Netanyahu won't be arrested in New York, pushing back on Mamdani
items.description: Israel's prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu is the target of an arrest warrant issued by the International Criminal Criminal Court related to the war on Gaza.

items.title: Archer stock rips 20% higher as company unveils military craft with Anduril
items.description: Archer and its eVTOL peers are racing for FAA certification to start flying their air taxis commercially.

items.title: Paramount and Warner Bros. merger hit with temporary restraining order
items.description: A California judge granted a temporary restraining order as part of the lawsuit brought by state attorneys general due to antitrust concerns.

items.title: South Carolina Republicans crowd field to succeed Sen. Lindsey Graham
items.description: Rep. Russell Fry became the latest South Carolina Republican to launch a campaign to succeed the late Sen. Lindsey Graham.

items.title: World Cup 2026 final in photos: Spain triumphs as football crowns a new champion
items.description: Spain reclaimed football's biggest prize on Sunday, defeating Argentina 1-0 after extra time in a tense World Cup final.

items.title: Man with airsoft rifles caused fireworks explosion outside NYC immigration court: MS NOW
items.description: The FBI told CNBC that an individual set off "an incendiary device" outside 26 Federal Plaza, which contains a U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement office.

items.title: AMD launches Helios, its first rack AI system to rival Nvidia, adding Microsoft as newest buyer
items.description: AMD is taking aim at Nvidia with Helios, its first AI rack system, and has landed Microsoft as a new customer, joining Meta, OpenAI and Oracle.

items.title: Boeing takes the right approach on new planes. Plus, a word of caution this earnings season
items.description: The Investing Club holds its "Morning Meeting" every weekday at 10:20 a.m. ET.

items.title: JetBlue wins Spirit slots at LaGuardia Airport, eyes move to Marine Air Terminal as airlines fight for space
items.description: JetBlue Airways won takeoff and landing slots for up to 12 round-trip flights at LaGuardia Airport.

items.title: What to know about testosterone replacement therapy as the U.S. military plans to test troops
items.description: While some research has shown benefits for patients who qualify, questions remain about the therapy's long-term risks and use among men without a medical need.

items.title: U.S. Treasury yields edge higher as Wall Street monitors Middle East tensions
items.description: The yield on the 10-year U.S. Treasury note — the key benchmark for U.S. government borrowing — rose over 1 basis point to 4.558%.

items.title: Alphabet stock pops on report it's developing a more efficient AI chip
items.description: The new AI chip, called "Frozen v2," would embed parts of Gemini's architecture directly into the silicon, according to the report.

items.title: Ryanair says 'no shortage' of travelers despite Iran war denting profit
items.description: Ryanair's first-quarter profit took a 34% hit as consumers delayed bookings due to the Middle East crisis, while struggling airlines face a "difficult winter."

items.title: UK's Andy Burnham becomes PM as Trump slams Britain as 'Poverty Stricken Disaster'
items.description: The prospect of Burnham, considered more left-leaning than Starmer, replacing the incumbent prime minister sent jitters through bond markets earlier this year.

items.title: Boeing CEO says company still needs 'a couple more years' to repair finances before new jet
items.description: CEO Kelly Ortberg signalled Boeing is focused more on stabilizing its existing business than rushing to develop a successor to its bestselling 737 MAX.

items.title: Voters wary of government ownership in companies as Trump administration takes equity stakes
items.description: About half of voters believe it isn't appropriate for the U.S. government to own stakes in companies, a new CNBC poll found.

items.title: Coinbase trading results may fall short of expectations, according to prediction markets
items.description: Speculators think in the company's second quarter earnings report, it will show trading volume fell for a third consecutive quarter.

items.title: 'Give me my money back': South Korean traders' leveraged SK Hynix, Samsung bets unravel after sell-off
items.description: South Korean retail investors suffer heavy losses after Samsung and SK Hynix shares reverse sharply.

items.title: TSMC is accelerating Arizona factory build-out to capitalize on AI 'megatrend,' CFO says
items.description: Speaking in an interview with CNBC, TSMC's Wendell Huang said the fresh investment comes on the back of robust customer demand.
